observations.recommendation:
We recommended the City Mayor to instruct the OIC-City Accountant to:

a) coordinate with the City GSO to evaluate the causes of the material
discrepancy of P23,174,224.14 and make reconciliation within 90 days from
receipt hereof to ascertain the fairness and reliability of the PPE balances in
the financial statements;
b) coordinate with the CEO for the documents necessary to record/reclassify
the aforementioned projects to their proper accounts and record the related
Depreciation Expense, Accumulated Depreciation and Accumulated Surplus
and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers
(JEVs) within 30 days from receipt thereof; and
We further recommended the City Mayor to direct the CEO to expedite the
processing of claims of the completed infrastructure projects by contract and
submit the same to the CAO to prevent the delayed recognition of the said
infrastructure assets in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a) LFC to analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last five years
and determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of
collectivity and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of Annual
Budget for the ensuing fiscal year;
b) City Treasurer to continue the daily monitoring of cash availability and
preparation of Status Report of Cash for submission to the Office of the
City Mayor and concerned department heads;
c) LFC to prepare the City’s plan for reduction of the cash deficit to a zero level as
soon as possible and submit the same to the Audit Team within 30 days from
receipt hereof;
d) City Budget Officer, OIC-City Accountant and City Treasurer to constantly and
regularly exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of
the City’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and
collected during the year to totally eliminate the cash deficiency of the City; and
e) LFC and other concerned department heads to strictly follow the Budget
Operation Manual for Local Government Units, 2016 Edition under Local Budget
Circular No. 112 of the Department of Budget and Management.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) City Treasurer to furnish the OIC-City Accountant a duly certified list of unpaid
taxes supported with details containing the name of the taxpayer and the
amount due to establish reliable balances of RPT and SET Receivable at the
beginning of each year;
b) City Treasurer and OIC-City Accountant to analyze and reconcile their records
to determine the deficiencies noted between their records and make
necessary adjustments within 60 days from receipt hereof; and
c) City Treasurer and City Administrator to coordinate and jointly exert extra
efforts in the collection of delinquent accounts and adopt necessary
measures to enable the maximum collection of unpaid taxes.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a) OIC-City Accountant, in coordination with respective offices, to prioritize the
determination and verification of the composition of the dormant asset and
payable accounts by tracing back and retrieving all possible information,
documents and records pertinent thereto within 60 days from receipt hereof; and
b) City Administrator to submit to the Audit Team the results of validation of the
dormant asset and payable accounts and the actions taken thereon duly
supported with relevant and sufficient documents within 60 days upon receipt
hereof, for evaluation.
We further recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned departments to
prepare the request for the write-off/adjustments of the receivable accounts and the
reversion of the long outstanding payables, with authority from Sangguniang
Panlungsod, both showing that the accounts have been dormant for a long time and
duly supported with complete documentation. Include applicable certifications and
proof of the actions and the extent of validation undertaken thereon.

We recommended the City Mayor to direct the City Accountant to:

a) prioritize the reconciliation of records and evaluation of the available documents
within 60 days from receipt thereof to establish the updated status of the
balances of the fund transfers particularly those grants with carry over
beginning balances;
b) coordinate with the implementing units to assess the needs for those
unexpended fund balances from NGAs. Should the funds be proven no longer
needed, remit immediately to the SA, but if still needed, request authority to use
the fund for other related projects of the City; and
c) prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries for the (i) transfer of the grants
and donations recognized in the GF book; and (ii) reclassification of the
misclassified livelihood financial assistance within 15 days from receipt thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the LDC to submit a ratified CDP and
LDIP that will specifically serve as the basis for the identification of development
projects to be prioritized during CY 2022 and applicable years thereafter.

We recommended that the City Mayor enforce timely project implementation by
requiring the City Engineer in coordination with City Planning and Development
Officer to:

a) conscientiously carry out detailed engineering addressing any issues on the
viability of the project sites to ensure that projects are implementation-ready
before they are posted for bidding;
b) thoroughly review and evaluate the Program of Works to ensure that project
designs and estimates are properly prepared and suit the needs of end-users
and that all phases of the projects are covered to minimize time extensions;
c) ensure full coordination with other government agencies, among others, DPWH,
DepEd and DENR including the community and private entities, to achieve
effective and more synchronized planning and implementation of projects; and
d) strictly monitor and supervise the status/progress of projects being
implemented.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer in coordination with the
City Planning and Development Officer to:

a) require the contractors to devise catch-up program within 15 days from receipt
hereof in order to fast track the completion of the projects with prolonged
implementation period to avoid rescission of the contract;
b) take appropriate actions to resolve the noted ROW issue within 20 days from
receipt hereof;
c) evaluate the viability of the infrastructure project which has yet to start for
possible contract termination and reprogramming of the funds appropriated
therein;
d) take appropriate actions for the delay in the completion of the project by
imposing liquidated damages of at least one-tenth of one percent of the cost
of the unperformed portion for every day of delay; and
e) intensify and perform regular monitoring and supervision and improve its
operations to accomplish the mandated targets it committed for the year to
ensure the immediate completion of the delayed projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to strictly follow the procedural guidelines in the one-time immediate cleansing of PPE and reconciliation of accounting records and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) prior to and during the conduct of inventory taking, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition for the non-existing/ missing PPEs, derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory were already undertaken by the City. The CEO also submitted the Accomplishment Report to the CAO to properly account and record the costs of the completed projects and the related Accumulated Depreciation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation for and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability were still not done by the concerned departments. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. General Services Officer to conduct inventory taking on a semestral basis and submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant on time; and b. Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of inventory accounts and prepare the corresponding adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if necessary, and submit to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Semestral inventory taking was conducted and report thereon was submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Adjusting entries were made under unnumbered JEV dated May 30, 2022. Some subsidiary ledger cards were being maintained already.
observations.reason: Unreconciled differences still existed in the CY 2022 records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Market Supervisor and the Permanent Market Committee to establish the total amount of unpaid market fees as of December 31, 2021 and submit the list to OIC-Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the receivable and accrued income and receivables from the market operations; and b. Market Supervisor, Municipal Administrator and the Municipal Treasurer to devise strategies to enforce collection of delinquent rental fees and facilitate effective management of market operations which may include issuance of demand letter, notice of suspension/eviction to stallholders, and imposition of corresponding legal sanctions to defaulting lessees.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Payment was sent by the Market Supervisor in CY 2022 informing the delinquent stallholders of their dues. He also awarded those vacant and revoked stalls to qualified applicants for additional revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all of the succeeding receipt of funds related to the disaster risk reduction and management program shall be recorded in the Trust Liability - DRRM account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 300-22-05-0015 and 300-22-05-0016, both dated May 31, 2022 were recorded. No funds related to the disaster risk reduction and management program were received in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all of the subsequent shares from the PCSO - Lotto Draws and STL of the Municipality is appropriately accounted as income and recorded using “Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Reclassification entries for the funds received from the PCSO were recorded under JEV No. 100-22-05-0120 dated May 31, 2022. The account was also being used by the City for the subsequent receipts of shares from PCSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her staff to prepare the Reconciliation of Statement of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities and the Reconciliation of Actual Amounts presented in SCBAA and in Statement of Financial Performance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 and IPSAS 24, and submit to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation statements were prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to monitor daily the continuing cash availability, prepare Status Report of Cash and submit daily to the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer; b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the practices of including the Continuing Appropriations and beginning balances with supporting breakdowns/computations in the Annual Budget without the computation showing the cash availability; c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor and limit expenditures within the total income collected and ensure that cash outflows do not exceed cash inflows; d. Municipal Treasurer to revisit and mobilize tax campaign strategies to boost collection capabilities; and e. Municipal General Services Officer and concerned departments to coordinate closely with the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant particularly before any procurement of goods and services is made.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer has been regularly monitoring the daily cash availability and the corresponding status reports were prepared and furnished to the concerned departments. Likewise, she has stopped certifying funds availability for disbursements unless there is sufficient cash; The City Budget Officer only included in the Annual Budget those Continuing Appropriations and beginning balances with supporting breakdowns/computations; The City has realized a surplus of P63,973,422.45 and net cash inflow for the year; Coordination with the various departments is being strictly implemented particularly before any procurement will be made; As of March 10, 2023, the City already collected the total amount of P487,092,986.63 which will be used to cover the obligations carried over to the period.
observations.reason: The City still failed to realize the projected revenue from tax and non-tax revenues and external sources for the year which greatly contributed to the incurrence of cash deficiency for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to stop inclusion of lump-sum projects and their appropriations in the Annual Budget and otherwise secure the prior approval/authority of the Sangguniang Bayan before charging an obligation against the lump-sum appropriation and before implementation of projects; and b. Local Finance Committee and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all of the details of projects as to its location and amount of appropriation are consistently presented in the Annual Budget and included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP).
observations.managementAction: The appropriations under the City Engineering Office for CY 2022 already included a detailed presentation for the locations. The projects were presented in the Annual Budget, but otherwise secure the prior approval/authority of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for CY 2022.
observations.reason: The appropriations were included in the Annual Budget but prior review/authority as required remained outstanding for some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances as of December 31, 2020 of RPT and SET Receivables with total delinquency tax reported by the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustment and to base the setting up of collectibles at the beginning of the year on the Certified List showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer; and b. OIC - Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile the noted real RPUs and determine the owners of identified real property units and submit to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording/adjustment or disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Some of the real property units were determined and corrected by the Office of the City Assessor and City Treasurer. The CTO already furnished the CAO a duly certified list of unpaid taxes supported with details containing the name of the taxpayer and the amount due. The City Treasurer and the OIC-City Accountant are on the process of analyzing and reconciling the records to make the necessary adjustment.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records between Accounting and Treasury were still ongoing and some records remained unreconciled as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for the road and its component, prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each road component at the end of the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 and disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements; b. General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format and maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component; c. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken; and d. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card respectively.
observations.managementAction: LRNLC was being prepared. Inventory taking and reports for some road networks were conducted and submitted. The City Engineer and his staff are preparing the complete description and segregation of road components. Some subsidiary ledger cards were being maintained already.
observations.reason: Data on the road lot components and complete description and cost segregation of road components were still not provided by the Office of the City Engineer due to lack of manpower and voluminous transactions to be traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure coordination by the Office of the Municipal Accountant towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials and relief goods for distribution and to prepare and submit the required Requisition Issue Slip and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
observations.managementAction: Supplies and materials procured in CY 2022 were recorded as Supplies and Inventory account by the Office of the Municipal Accountant. Inventory taking was conducted for some properties. Some records were being prepared.
observations.reason: Supplies and materials procured and relief goods distribution records were not completely prepared

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the cash advance voucher and supporting documents for confidential expenses were submitted on time to the Audit Team within seven days from the release of check.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance voucher and supporting documents for confidential expenses were being submitted on time to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members scrutinize and examine properly the sources of appropriation of Annual Budget before approval and ensure that the LDRRMF funds will be appropriated and planned pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and Sangguniang Panlungsod were ensuring that the LDRRM funds will be appropriated and planned pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Fund was still not optimally utilized in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to maintain the database/list of families that need cash assistance in crisis situation and ensure the immediate preparation of prior payroll with qualified and eligible beneficiaries/payees in subsequent emergency subsidy program / cash assistance funded by the municipality and/or national government.
observations.managementAction: Database/list of families consisting of 8,861 beneficiaries was maintained and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her staff to prepare and submit the Reconciliation of Statement of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the OIC-Municipal Accountant submitted the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities for the General Fund and Special Education Fund and informed that no reconciliation was prepared in the Trust Fund book. Nevertheless, she promised that the required reconciliations in CY 2022 and succeeding years will be submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Preparation and submission of reconciliation for CY 2022 within the prescribed period was not given much attention by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to strictly
follow the procedural guidelines in the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts such as the (a)
preliminary activities prior to and during the conduct of inventory taking, (b) reconciliation
of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, (c) disposition for
the non-existing/missing PPEs, (d) derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without
available record of accountability, and (e) accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes
to the Financial Statements, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. General Services Officer to conduct inventory taking of all inventory accounts on a
semestral basis and submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant on time; and
b. Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of Inventory accounts and
prepare the corresponding adjusting Journal Entry Voucher, if necessary, and submit
to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Market Supervisor and the Permanent Market Committee to establish the total amount
of unpaid market fees as of December 31, 2021 and submit the list to the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the accrued income and receivables
from the stallholders who operate and occupied the space provided and subsequently
follow the accrual method of accounting in recording the income from market
operation; and
b. Market Supervisor, Municipal Administrator and Municipal Treasurer to devise
strategies to enforce collection of delinquent rental fees and facilitate effective
management of market operations which may include issuance of demand letter, notice
of suspension/eviction to stallholders, and imposition of corresponding legal
sanctions to defaulting lessees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that all of the succeeding receipt of funds related to the disaster risk reduction and
management program shall be recorded in the Trust Liability - DRRM account pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all of the subsequent shares from the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office
(PCSO) - Lotto Draws and Small Town Lottery (STL) of the Municipality is appropriately
accounted as income and recorded using “Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her staff
to prepare the Reconciliation of Statement of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities
and the Reconciliation of Actual Amounts presented in SCBAA and in Statement of
Financial Performance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 and IPSAS 24, and submit
to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to monitor daily the cash availability, prepare Status Report of
Cash and submit daily to the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer. Likewise, stop certifying funds availability for disbursements when the
same is not available;
b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the
practices of including the Continuing Appropriations and Savings/Beginning Balance
as a source of appropriation in the Annual Budget without the computation showing the
cash availability;
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor and limit
expenditures within the total income collected and ensure that cash outflows do not
exceed the cash inflows;
d. Municipal Treasurer to revisit and mobilize tax campaign strategies to boost collection
capabilities in order to increase revenue resources; and
e. Municipal General Services Officer and concerned Department Heads to coordinate
closely with the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant before any procurement of goods and services is made.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, OIC-Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to stop inclusion of lumpsum
appropriations in the Annual Budget, but instead present the project in detail,
otherwise secure prior approval/authority from the Sangguniang Bayan before
charging an obligation against the lumpsum appropriation and before implementation
of projects; and
b. Local Finance Committee and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all of the
details of projects as to its location and amount of appropriation presented in the
Annual Budget are consistent and included in the approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive inventory taking and immediately reconcile the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) results with the accounting records and any differences should be identified and traced whether these are missing or unserviceable properties;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledger cards for each group of PPEs indicating every details of the properties pursuant to Section 269, Volume I, of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and reconcile the acquisition cost of all PPEs presented in the lapsing schedule and financial statements as of year-end; and
c. General Services Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPEs of the municipality with complete description and details for proper monitoring.
observations.managementAction:
The General Services Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant, being the Chairman and Vice-
Chairman of the Inventory Committee, respectively, informed that they will comply with the audit
recommendation. They replied that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 were already
put into action in CY 2022 as evidenced by the following:
a. In view of the changes in the personnel of the Municipality, the Inventory Committee was
reconstituted by virtue of Administrative Order No. 02 issued by the Municipal Mayor on June
1, 2022.
b. The Municipal Administrator issued a memorandum to the members of the Inventory
Committee on June 3, 2022 enjoining them to attend a Webinar on one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances of government agencies to be conducted by the Government Financial
Management Innovator Circle (GFMIC) Inc. on June 15, 2022.
c. The existing Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment was provided by the General
Services Office to each department for initial identification of serviceable and unserviceable
PPEs.
d. The General Services Officer already coordinated with the District Supervisor of Department
of Education – Calaca District, barangays and offices of municipal government regarding the
inventory taking of all PPEs.
e. The General Services Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant drafted the initial guidelines and
procedures for Physical Inventory Planning (PIP).
observations.reason:
Unavailability of records.
Subsidiary Ledger cards for each group of PPEs indicating every details of the properties pursuant to Section 269, Volume I, of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs is on-going.
Unavailability of source documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly Certified List showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due as of December 31, 2020 and the amounts collectible for the CY 2021 and the years thereafter and furnish to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year;
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances as of December 31, 2020 and effect the necessary adjustment and base the setting up of collectibles at the beginning of the year on the Certified List showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer; and
c. OIC-Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile the noted real property units reported as not tie-up and determine the owners of the unidentified real property units and submit to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording/adjustment or disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer facilitated the preparation and submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of taxpayers and the tax due as of December 31, 2020.
The Municipal Accountant is conducting reconciliation of collectibles.
Some of the real property units reported as not tie-up and unidentified real RPUs were already determined and corrected by the Office of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation is still ongoing. For continuation in CY 2022.
Data not provided by the Office of the Municipal Engineer.
Verification and determination of owners of unidentified RPUs and no tie-up requires participation of the real property taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: • prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures; • prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format; and • maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component.
b. General Services Officer to: • prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format; and • maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component.
c. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken; and
d. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer and his staff is currently preparing the complete project description and cost segregation of road components.
The General Services Office conducted inventory taking of road networks and included in the submitted RPCPPE.
The Office of the Municipal Budget prepared the road component list.
observations.reason:
Data not provided by the Office of the Municipal Engineer.
Lack of manpower who will prepare the report on road networks.
Road component not yet provided by the Office of the Municipal Engineer.
There were records ongoing in the General Services Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the properties, plant and equipment and completed infrastructure projects funded out of Trust Receipts/Fund and subsequently transfer the same to the General Fund books and effect the corresponding depreciation in accordance with PPSAS No. 17.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant prepared the JEVs recognizing the completed properties and recorded depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of accounts payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and inspected, pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98, as amended and clarified under BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accounting office recognized the withheld taxes and adjusted accounts payable according to BIR guidance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of the procured items pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs. We also recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant submit to the COA Audit Team the copy of distribution list and/or proof of acknowledgements of supplies and materials and welfare goods and that such goods were properly distributed to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
The General Services Officer informed that they are already using the stock card in CY 2022 for the monitoring and inventory of stocks in accordance with the provision of Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs. In addition, her office is in close coordination with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of inventory account.
The Municipal Accountant informed that adjustments for the Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers account and Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution account was already made in CY 2022.
observations.reason:
The preparation of Requisition Issue Slip and Summary of Materials Issued were not prepared. For implementation in CY 2022.
Some of the supplies and welfare goods were not yet distributed and documentation incomplete at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant to: • recognize in the books the unutilized balance of ER 1-94 Funds; • record all subsequent shares of the Municipality from ER 1-94 Funds under the Share from National Wealth account in the General Fund book; and • prepare and maintain separate registry by the General Fund Office for ER 1-94 Funds and separate registries for the Share from National Wealth funds pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 03-01-2017.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries and reclassified the related amounts. The adjusting entries were taken up under JEV Nos. 100-21-06 and 300-21-06, both dated June 8, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the practices of disbursing the ER 1-94 Funds without the approved appropriation of the Sangguniang Bayan;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the appropriation/budget funded out of ER 1-94 Funds and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan for their appropriate action in accordance with Section 294 of RA 7160 and ensure that all subsequent disbursements were in accordance with the approved appropriations; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain separate account in the General Fund and separate registries for the Share from National Wealth or ER 1-94 Funds pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer prepared the budget appropriation funded out of ER 1-94 funds and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan. A separate registry was maintained.
observations.reason: The budget/appropriation funded out of ER 1-94 Funds in CY 2021 was included in the Annual Budget/Supplemental Budget for CY 2021 and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review and determine if the claims on the noted Accounts Payable were still valid, ensure that all recorded payables were supported with the required documentary requirements and, drop from the books all payables considered invalid claims and not properly supported with necessary documents by seeking the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant reviewed and ensured that all Accounts Payable were supported with documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the three disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents totalling P4,485,580.00 to the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 39 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The three disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were submitted to the Commission on Audit along with notarized acknowledgement of receipt of the supplier, notarized affidavit of loss, certified copy of issued checks and snapshot.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned employees and the approving officers to refund the honoraria paid by the Municipality in excess of their 25 percent basic monthly salary totalling P137,937.00 and the amount paid as honoraria to the employee assigned in the General Services Office totalling P75,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P137,937.00 and P75,000.00 was already refunded by the concerned employees as of year-end.

The disbursement vouchers for the confidential fund cash advances were recorded and liquidated. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive inventory taking and immediately
reconcile results with the accounting records and any differences should be
identified and traced whether these are missing or unserviceable properties;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each group of
PPEs indicating every details of the properties pursuant to Section 269, Volume I,
of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and reconcile the acquisition cost of all
PPEs presented in the lapsing schedule and financial statements as of year-
end; and
c. General Services Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPEs of the
municipality with complete description and details for proper monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly
Certified List showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due as of December
31, 2020 and the amounts collectibles for the CY 2021 and the years thereafter and
furnish to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year;
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances as of December 31, 2020 of RPT
and SET Receivables with total delinquency tax reported by the Municipal
Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustment and base the setting up of
collectibles at the beginning of the year on the Certified List showing the name of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the
Municipal Treasurer; and
c. OIC-Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile the noted
real property units reported as not tie-up and determine the owners of the
unidentified real property units and submit to the Municipal Accountant for proper
recording/adjustment or disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road and its
component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope
protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures;
• prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each road component at the end of the year in accordance
with COA Circular 2015-008; and
• disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
b. General Services Officer to:
• prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format; and
• maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
component.
c. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of
road components of every road project undertaken; and
d. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to secure from the Municipal
Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of
every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in
the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw
the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the unrecorded properties, plant and equipment
and the completed infrastructure projects in the books and its corresponding
depreciation pursuant to Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No.
17.

recognize in the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of
accounts payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and
accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and
inspected pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98, as amended and clarified
under BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.

General Services Officer to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of

materials and relief goods for distribution and consumption pursuant to
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

to the constituents of the Municipality.

Operating Activities pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004.

Trust Fund Book

Cash in Bank – LCCA ₱17,936,149.19

General Fund Book

Separate Account in the General Fund and separate Registries for the Share from

budget/appropriation funded out of ER 1-94 Funds from the executive office of the
