Financial Statements on the actual usage of each parcels of land based on the report
to be prepared by the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer;
d. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor include in the appropriations
the titling of land owned by the Municipal Government; and
e. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to process the necessary documents
and register the land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare
the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the recorded Construction in
Progress into appropriate PPE account and provide the corresponding depreciation
expenses pursuant to NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to transfer the corresponding cash of unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in the Trust Fund book at the end of the year, and recognize in the Trust
Fund book using the Trust Liability-DRRM account, if actual cash cannot be
transferred at the end of the year, record the liability of the General Fund to the Trust
Fund book and its corresponding receivable account in the Trust Fund book.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of LDRRMC,
ensure that the unexpended LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund of Trust Fund book
shall be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and
management activities.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and
Sangguniang Bayan stop the reversion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF which
are within the next five years period in the Trust Fund book as required under Section
21 of RA 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Principal Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to update immediately the employees
profile specifically the basic salary and its effectivity on the GSIS system and ensure
that the premium contributions of all municipal officials and employees are properly
withheld and remitted to the GSIS pursuant to Section 14 of IRR of RA 8291;
b. Human Resource Management and Municipal Accountant to base the withholding
loan amortizations on the Billing Statements;
c. Principal Agency Authorized Officer coordinate with the Electronic Remittance File
(ERF) officer and immediately act on the Reconciliation Billing Issues provided by the
GSIS and ;
d. Municipal Accountant to adjust the noted misposting on the Subsidiary Ledger and
ensure the correct posting of all subsequent withholding and remittances of
premiums and loan amortization to its appropriate Subsidiary Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PRAISE Committee to strictly
observe the basic policies stated in the Agency PRAISE and CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 1 s. 2001, and ensure that the general and specific objectives of the
program were achieved.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan Members, and
PRAISE Committee and other officials who approve and authorize the transaction and
the recipients of the incentive to refund the amount of ₱4,900,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the practice of hiring the private lawyer
or special legal officer/consultant without the written concurrence of the
Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 86-255, as amended by COA
Circular Nos. 95-011 and 98-002 and affirmed under Supreme Court Decision GR No.
140563. Consequently, require the refund of the payments totaling ₱561,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Human Resource Management Officer to instigate a standard hiring process for
engaging in Job Order and identify the specific duties and responsibilities of
each JOs and its corresponding rates based on identified requirements and
necessity of a particular office or department;
b. Human Resource Management Officer and the Local Finance Committee ensure that
the payment of wages to Job Order workers is in accordance with the minimum
wage rate; and
c. Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure the
proper and complete documentation before payment of wages of the Job Order
workers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractors and require them to refund the
amount of deficiency noted by the COA TAS; and
b. Municipal Accountant to deduct from the claims of contractors the liquidated
damages for non-completion of projects within the contract time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and
Human Resource Management Officer to provide appropriation and hire for the three
mandatory regular staff in the Local DRRM Office in compliance with RA 10121 and
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan ensure that the mandatory
positions in the Local DRRM Office had the corresponding appropriations in the
Annual Budget of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Officer to prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIP
specifying in details the programs, projects and activities funded out of unexpended
LDRRMF in the Trust Fund book, and submit to the local sanggunian through the
LDRRMC, pursuant to RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare an accurate Report on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund and disclose reliable information on the Notes to Financial
Statements consistent with the report provided by the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Development Council to identify clearly the priority programs, projects and
activities and avoid changes of planned PPAs and refrain from the usual practice of
using lumpsum appropriations under 20 percent Development Fund for
incorporation in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget for the year; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer to secure approval from the
Sangguniang Bayan before implementation of projects presented in generic terms
or with lumpsum appropriation in the approved appropriation ordinance.
We also recommended that the Executive Committee of Municipality’s Development
Council and the Sangguniang Bayan ensure that any changes made on the approved
priority programs, projects and activities funded out of 20 percent Development Fund
shall undergo proper consultation and discussion with all the members or majority of
the Local Development Council supported with minutes of the meeting.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of
20 percent Development Fund pursuant to Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the GAD Focal Point
System and its Technical Working Group to establish and maintain GAD Database
pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01;
b. GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to
conduct the gender analysis on the programs and projects of the Municipality
using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool pursuant
to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-1; and
c. Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Point System ensure that at least five (5)
percent of the programs, projects and activities of the Municipality’s total budget
are gender-responsive pursuant to Section 43 of Republic Act No. 9710.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to: a) adjusted/recorded the payments of mobilization fees under the account Advances to Contractors instead of Advances to Construction in Progress account for payment of mobilization fee amounting to ₱11,296,080.90 and b) make appropriate accounting entries for a fair presentation of the property, plant and equipment accounts, as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer replied that they will comply with the audit recommendations and informed that the Inventory Committee started to conduct inventory taking of properties.
observations.reason: Absence of PPE Ledger Cards and/or any schedule of PPE with corresponding records hinder the reconciliation of the account in the Accounting and General Education Fund records. Reiterated in Observation No.2, Part II, page 40.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Treasurer to provide the duly certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for CY 2019 and years thereafter; b) Municipal Accountant to collection of receivables based on gross amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer started the preparation of certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for CY 2019 and years thereafter; Municipal Treasurer started the monitor the real property tax list of taxpayers and Municipal Accountant will prepare and monitor the collection of receivables based on the total collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Not This was not given much attention by the Office of the Municipal Accountant. For implementation in CY 2020. Reiterated in Observation No.1, Part II, page 38. Also, reliability of the accounts could not be ascertained due to discrepancies with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office record hence, the financial reconciliation could not be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Review the validity of the Accounts Payable payable which are outstanding for more than two years. If found to have no claims against the local government unit, seek the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus; b) Conduct periodic review to ensure that the account consist of valid and legitimate claims against the Government; and c) Record Obligations/ payables to determine the claim of contractors when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted the accounts payable which are outstanding for more than two years in order to fairly present the cash in bank and affected accounts reflected in the books and reflected in the unreleased checks as of December 31, 2018 and made the necessary adjustments. Also, checks issued were claimed by the concerned payees and appropriate action was undertaken. The Municipal Accountant will continue the periodic review and will determine the validity of the claims. The Municipal Accountant adjusted accounts payable per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 2019-06-0008 and 2019-06-0009 both dated June 30, 2019 and per JEV No. 2019-12-0181 dated December 27, 2019.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records hinders determination of the causes of abnormal balances. Some accounts were reverted; however, proper documentation and full verification of the remaining abnormal balances are still on-going as of year-end. Reiterated in Observation No.3, Part II, page 41.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office List of Unreleased Checks with check nos. to VLP Region IV-A, LPPDPCI Communications addressed to the local government units which will enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts adjustments to be reflected in the books and presented in the financial statements; and b) Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for accurate and reliable cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Checks to VLP Region IV-A, LLPDPCI Communications addressed to the local government units were determined and appropriate action were undertaken. The Municipal Treasurer sent written notices to the payees for the existence of stale checks and the said checks were claimed by the concerned payees. The Municipal Accountant reconciled the bank items and effected adjustments in the books per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 2019-06-0008 and 2019-06-0009 both dated June 30, 2019 and JEV No. 2019-12-0181 dated December 27, 2019.
observations.reason: Checks issued were claimed by the concerned payees; adjustments were effected for some items. However, reconciling items on the bank account were not all determined and adjusted in a timely manner; hence, some reconciling items remained in the books. Also, the Municipal Treasurer did send notices but some GAFMIS/PS-DBM related procedures were not fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances and to determine the necessary adjustments/corrections. Moreover, ensure that all accounts are in their normal balances and fairly presented in the resulting financial statements. Further, the Municipal Accountant must exercise due diligent and care in the performance of her duties and responsibilities to ensure the non-recurrence of the abnormal balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that due diligence and care was observed by her office in the performance of their duties and responsibilities. She also informed that adjustments were effected and reconciliations were undertaken to determine the causes of abnormal balances; some accounts were reverted and adjusted in the books.
observations.reason: Abnormal balances occurred in previous years and are still subject for review and determination of its causes in order to adjust the balances presented in the financial statements. The existing abnormal balances were not all completely resolved due to lack of full documentation and time to validate.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to properly withhold creditable income taxes of 5 per cent or 10 per cent, whichever is applicable, from the honoraria paid to employees of national government agencies and remit the same to the BIR through BIR Form no. 0605 on a monthly basis and BIR Form No. 1601-EQ on a quarterly basis, to help the accelerate the collection of income.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer implemented withholding of taxes on honoraria paid to national government employees starting May 2019 and remit the same to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer: a) Meticulously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at the lowest price availed; and b) Comply with the regulations that ordinary and regular office supplies involving amounts below the threshold should be first be procured from DBM - PS. Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to only items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus, in case shopping is resorted to, the claim should be supported with a certification from the PS-DBM that the items to be procured from other sources are not available in their stock.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the General Services Officer prepared the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan for common supplies and equipment for CY 2019. The Municipality procured the ordinary and regular office supplies from PS-DBM three times in CY 2019. Delivery Receipts provided by the PS-DBM indicate that other items not included in the DR were not currently available in the Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: a) Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Coordinator to justify the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and/or present any document like Suspension Order/ Time Extension to the Office of the Auditor; and b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claim of contractors of the above-mentioned projects when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator coordinated with the contractors and submitted justifications on delayed completion and submitted the status of works and suspension orders or time extensions to the Audit Team. The construction of three storey nine classroom building at Calaca Central Elementary School had liquidated damages imposed and deducted on the payment to the contractor per check no. 544956 dated September 13, 2019.
observations.reason: Appropriate action on remaining projects is to be undertaken by the concerned municipal officials in CY 2020 for the remaining projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Management to: a) coordinate with the contractors and require them to refund the amount of deficiency noted by the COA TAS; and b) deduct from the claims of contractors the liquidated damages for non-completion of projects within the contract time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management coordinated with the contractors and required the contractors to take necessary remedial measures and refund the noted deficiencies. Liquidated damages were imposed and deducted on payments for some projects (e.g. three storey nine classroom building at Calaca Central Elementary School and other projects) per applicable checks dated during the year.
observations.reason: The status of compliance and corrective measures on other projects are yet to be submitted to the Audit Team. Some liquidated damages and deductions were applied, while other remedial measures are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to scrutinize the contractor’s manpower on all projects to be awarded and not on an individual cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period; and b) Municipal Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and its TWG ensured that contractors are complying with the manpower requirements of all projects implemented by the Municipality. The Municipal Engineer informed that his office will closely monitor all projects implemented and will report on the quarterly GPPAs submitted to the Commission on Audit. Individual status of work per project was prepared for some projects and reported to the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Individual status of work per project was not made for all projects due to lack of manpower. Monitoring was improved but still not fully comprehensive for all projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Market Supervisor to: a) devise plans and strategies to facilitate effective management of market operations which may include issuance of demand letters, notice of suspension/ eviction to stallholders who habitually/ continuously violate the provisions of the lease contract; b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent market rental fees and effect the imposition of surcharge and corresponding legal sanctions to defaulting lessees as provided in the lease contract; c) facilitate the execution and update issuance of new lease contract with the occupants/ tenants in the market; and d) Municipal Accountant to establish the appropriate receivable account to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Market Supervisor coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer and enforced collection which resulted in a decrease of delinquent market fees from ₱5,781,408.00 as of December 31, 2018 to ₱1,761,110.00 as of December 31, 2019. The Market Supervisor reminded the occupants/ tenants to pay their market fees and effect the collection. However, issuance of updated Contract of Lease with the market occupants/ tenants was not given much attention by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that the list of delinquent stall rentals as of December 31, 2019 was not provided to her office before the finalization of the financial statements.
observations.reason: Collection efforts were enforced resulting in the decrease of delinquent accounts. However, issuance of updated lease contracts and timely provision of delinquent lists to the Municipal Accountant were not completely done; hence, some actions remain pending and the receivable account was not properly presented prior to finalization of financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to include all the eligible and qualified senior citizens in order of priority under the updated list of eligible senior citizen in submission to the DSWD Field Office. Conduct Validation and monitoring of the beneficiaries to determine their situation and the utilization of the stipend for the full implementation of Social Pension Program of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) conducted validation and continuous monitoring of the senior citizen beneficiaries. The updated list was continuously submitted to the DSWD Field Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Development Council to include only projects that are feasible and ready for implementation with properly planned timing of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan; and b) Municipal Budget Officer to secure approval from the Sangguniang Bayan before implementation of projects presented in generic terms or with lumpsum appropriation in the approved appropriation ordinance. Also, ensure that liquidated damages are imposed where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials replied that most of the Punong Barangays were consulted about the reversion and changes of the programs, projects and activities; however, the secretariat failed to take note the minutes of meeting. The noted projects were bidded and awarded during the year 2019. They stated that they will comply with all of the audit recommendations. Liquidated damages were imposed on some delayed projects and deducted on payments where applicable.
observations.reason: Some projects were completed and liquidated damages imposed on certain delayed contracts; however, proper consultation, documentation (minutes), and full compliance with the required pre-approval process for lump-sum appropriations were not fully observed, resulting in unfinished projects and internal control weaknesses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Commission on Audit; b) Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-MOOE and QRF for CY 2018 amounting to ₱7,651,379.00 in the Trust Fund books; and c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect necessary journal entries in the accounting books and for the reversion of the unutilized LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books which remained unutilized for more than five years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management responded that the MDRRMC prepared and submitted the Program of Utilization for the unexpended LDRRMF in the Trust Fund book for the CYs 2016 to 2019 amounting to ₱40,998,611.70 on January 20, 2020 and subsequently approved by the Sangguniang Bayan per Resolution No. 20-06 for utilization for Taal Volcano Relief Operation and other works. The unspent balance of LDRRMF for CY 2018 amounting to ₱10,352,252.00 was transferred in the Trust Fund book per Journal Entry Voucher No. 2019-01-0005 dated January 22, 2019. The Municipality reverted balances in trust fund in CY 2019 per required journal entries for certain years as reported.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports were not prepared timely in CY 2019; some transfers and reversions were made but some reversion of unutilized LDRRMF in Trust Fund books spanning more than five years were effected without full compliance with Section 21 of RA 10121, and additional journal adjustments were required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to: a) Evaluate and review the SEF budget with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of priority programs and projects suitable to the needs of the public schools in the Municipality and come up with a Procurement/Investment Plan to support the SEF budget; and b) Approve the SEF budget that is presented by program, activity and project (P/A/P), by allotment and by class and by allotment using SEF Budget Preparation No. 1 pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 2017 dated January 19, 2017 for strategic and efficient utilization of resources.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board evaluated the SEF budget and the Local School Board reviewed the programs, projects and activities funded out of Special Education Fund such as construction of buildings and other facilities amounting to ₱116,317,000.00 and repair and maintenance for Brigada Eskwela amounting to ₱8,500,000.00. The SEF budget for CY 2019 was reviewed by the Local School Board but presentation by program/class and allotment needs further improvement. The Local School Board will comply with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1.
observations.reason: The SEF budget was reviewed and some major projects were identified and funded; however, the presentation and detailing by program, activity and project (P/A/P) and by allotment per school was not fully complied with, requiring further action by the Local School Board and Municipal Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC, Human Resource Management Officer to: a) Regularly reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies; b) Identify the cause/s of the remittance and make the necessary adjustments; c) Effect payroll deduction on loan amortization of employees who availed loan with GSIS and remit the same pursuant to Section 14.2, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 8291 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and d) Determine remittances which exceeded the amount deducted from payroll and make liable the concerned municipal employee who prepared and approved the schedule of remittances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that the noted unremitted balances were already acted upon and reconciled. The Human Resource Management Officer coordinated with GSIS and updated premium contributions and loan amortizations; unremitted contributions were checked, verified and properly remitted to the GSIS. The Municipal Accountant reclassified and corrected the noted errors in posting in the Subsidiary Ledger per Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2020-09-0180 dated September 14, 2020.
observations.reason: Reconciliations and corrective adjustments were undertaken starting in mid-2019 and in 2020; however, some remittances and posting errors occurred in prior months and required time to fully reconcile and adjust. Complete resolution and further monitoring were to be undertaken in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to provide reconciliation of the difference noted and make appropriate adjustments in their respective records, if necessary, in the course of reconciliation of records made by the said offices in order to arrive at reliable balances of PPE accounts, including the proper accounting entries for mobilization fees for a fair presentation of accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY 2019 and years thereafter;
b) Municipal Accountant to record collection of receivables based on gross amounts of taxes collected and set-up receivables based on the total collectibles for the year as provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and
c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Review the validity of the unpaid obligations or accounts payable which are outstanding for more than two years. If found to have no claims against the local government unit, seek the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus;
b) Conduct periodic review to ensure that the Accounts Payable account consist of valid and legitimate claims against the Municipal Government; and
c) Record obligations/payables to legitimate creditors who have satisfactorily performed/ delivered infrastructure goods and services to the Municipality and whose claims are found valid and supported with the necessary documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to i) prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks in order to fairly present the cash in bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements; and ii) for the checks which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of new checks; and
b) Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to hasten the request for write-off adjustments of the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit duly supported with the list of available records pertaining to the accounts as to the extent of validation made on the accounts, reasons why the records/documents/schedules/ could not be located and the number of years the accounts have been dormant.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant initiate a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances and make proper adjustments/corrections. Moreover, ensure that all accounts are in their normal balances and fairly presented in the resulting financial statements.

Further, the Municipal Accountant must exercise due diligent and care in the performance of her duties and responsibilities to ensure the non-recurrence of the abnormal balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly withhold creditable income taxes of 5 per cent or 10 per cent, whichever is applicable, from the honoraria paid to employees of national government agencies and remit the same to the BIR through BIR Form No. 0605 on a monthly basis and BIR Form No. 1601-EQ on a quarterly basis, to help the government accelerate the collection of income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to:
a. Meticulously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at the lowest price is availed; and
b. Comply with the regulations that procurement of ordinary and regular office supplies involving amounts below the threshold should be first be procured from DBM. Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to only for items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus, in case shopping is resorted to, the claim should be supported with a certification from the PS-DBM that the items to be procured are not available in their stock.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to provide justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and/or present any document like Suspension Order/Time Extension which must be duly approved by the Municipal Mayor; and
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claim of contractors of the above-mentioned projects, when necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractors to make the necessary action on the defective work of the completed projects and submit to the audit team status of compliance for submission to the TAS or require them to refund the amount of deficiency noted by the COA TAS; and
b) Municipal Accountant to deduct from the claims of contractors the liquidated damages for non-completion of projects within the contract time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to scrutinize the contractor’s manpower on all projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period. This is in order to require contractor to augment its manpower to be utilized in the project awarded to it or to disqualify it from award of several contracts beyond its capacity; and
b) Municipal Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual status report per project to be submitted to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Market Supervisor to devise plans and strategies to facilitate effective management of market operations which may include issuance of demand letters, notice of suspensions/eviction to stallholders who habitually/continuously violate the provisions of the Revenue Code.
b. Market Supervisor to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent rental fees and effect the imposition of surcharge and corresponding legal sanctions to defaulting lessees as provided in the lease contract;
c. Market Supervisor to facilitate the execution and update issuance of new lease contract with the occupants/tenants in the market; and
d. Municipal Accountant to establish the appropriate receivable account to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to include all the eligible and qualified senior citizens in order of priority provided under the DSWD Administrative Order No. 15 series of 2010 dated December 13, 2010 in the updated list of eligible senior citizens for submission to the DSWD Field Office. Likewise, conduct quarterly validation and monitoring to the beneficiaries to determine their situation and the utilization of the stipend for the full implementation of Social Pension Program of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal Development Council, require that the Council to include only projects that are feasible and ready for implementation with properly planned timing of implementation in its Annual Investment Plan.

We also recommended that the Management instruct the concerned officers to justify the non-imposition of liquidated damages despite delay on the project completion and to submit to the Office of the Auditor suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and impose the liquidated damages based on the provisions cited on the foregoing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council;
b) Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE and QRF for CY 2018 amounting to ₱7,651,379.00 in the Trust Fund books; and
c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to effect the necessary journal entries in the accounting books and Registry of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations, respectively, for the reversion of LDRRMF amounting to ₱13,074,034.81 lodged in the trust fund books which remained unutilized for more than five years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to:
a) Evaluate and review the SEF budget with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of priority programs and projects suitable to the needs of the public schools in the Municipality and come up with a Procurement/Investment Plan to support the SEF budget; and

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and
continuous failure of the LGU to correct such deficiencies, we recommended that
the LGU consider the following:
 The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all the
LGU’s properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain
complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.
 Immediately comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines
provided in the Commission on Audit Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015 to fairly present the LGU’s financial statements.

We recommended that the property, plant and equipment Road
Networks of P89,659,825.46 and Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment of
P2,899,000.00 be provided with depreciation to fairly present the
municipality’s financial statements as of December 31, 2017. We further
recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the reported Property,
Plant and Equipment in view of the discrepancy of the PPE reported expenses
in the Cash Flow Statement and the total additions to the property, plant and
equipment in CY 2017 of P803,633.49.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request from the
municipality’s depository banks statement of account every end of the month for
the preparation of monthly reconciliation statement pursuant to Section 74 of
Presidential Decree (PD 1445) and COA Circular No. 96-011.We further
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
check and the Municipal Accountant to make proper adjustment in the
municipality’s books of accounts to fairly present the financial statements as of
December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and revise Note 30
of the Notes to Financial Statements of the financial statements of the
Municipality of Calaca for CY 2017 and effect necessary corrections to show the
correct amounts in each elements of Note 27 or Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows
from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) CY 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 Instruct the Municipal Assessor to cause the production of all legal
documents for the issuance of Tax Declaration in the name of the
Municipality and the Land Registration Authority/Register of Deeds for
the issuance of an appropriate Certificate of Title/Transfer Certificate of
Title (CT/TCT) for the recently acquired six (6) real properties;
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services
Officer to strictly comply with the requirements on the procurement of
land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
pertinent provision of Presidential Decree No. 1529, ensure that all
claims against government funds be supported with all the required
documentations, refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with
incomplete supporting documents and submit all the lacking documents
listed above; and
 Require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure the
TCT of the municipality’s real properties.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant transfer the balance of
Preparedness/Mitigation Fund of P265,038.00to the Special Trust Fund under
the account Trust Liability – DRRM in the Trust Fund books and prepare
monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund and submit a copy to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the
previously cited auditing and accounting laws and rules and regulations. Submit
the disbursement voucher and supporting documents to the auditor within the
prescribed period to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting
Municipal Treasurer and the Collecting Officers to consider the prescribed
guidelines on the frequency of depositing collection in the authorized depository
bank particularly where the amount involved is material to prevent exposure of
the funds from possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to remit the real property tax share to all the barangays to
augment the limited financial resources and to allow the immediate use of the
fund for regular expenses and priority projects.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to strictly
comply with the provision of Article 362 (b) of the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of the
Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of Commission on Audit, Department of Budget
and Management, Department of the Interior and Local Government,
Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense dated
January 8, 2015 prescribing the guidelines for the documentary requirements of
the granting of cash advances for confidential expenses; submit its disbursement
vouchers to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven (7) days after
the release of the check covered with transmittal letter enumerating the
supporting documents in a sealed envelope and submit a copy of an ordinance or
administrative order for the organization of the Municipal Peace and Order
Council.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit
relevant documents to support the excess of P450,000.00 as against the total
budget of P1,000,000.00 for Confidential Expenses.

We recommended that the:
a) Local Chief Executive formulate Implementing Guidelines and clear-cut
policies on the implementation of the Medical Assistance
Program/Orange Card Program pursuant to Section 7 of Sanggunian
Bayan Ordinance No. 14-264 for the effective and efficient
implementation of the program; and
b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office reconcile the basis of
per capita incomeused on the Health Card System vis-a-vis Section
4.2.1 of Sanggunian Bayan Ordinance No. 14-264 to prevent the
misclassification of the category of the cardholders entitling them the full
potential/benefits of the Orange Card Program, otherwise submit
updated Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance and/or Resolution for the new
basis used as well as for the updated category.
c) Assign orange card number to every valid health card beneficiaries
whether they are the registered orange card user or they are their
dependents to easily and accurately identify and validate their identities
and insure that they are the qualified beneficiaries of Medical Assistance
Program/Orange Card Program.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant the immediate
submission of the thirteen (13) Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting
documents to avoid the suspension and/or disallowance of the same and the
strict compliance with the provisions of Section 100 of PD No. 1445;
b. Sangguniang Bayan propose the passage of an ordinance establishing a
municipal scholarship program, specifying, among others, the composition of
the Scholarship Board/Committee, the eligibility requirements, qualifying
maximum family income level of applicants to ensure that only poor
students, as determined under Republic Act 8425, otherwise known as the
Social Reform and Poverty Alleviation Act dated 11 December 1997, to avail
of the scholarship, in addition to other requirements such as the minimum
grade, expenses to be covered by the scholarship, the specific degree courses,
the college or university to enroll to, the obligations of the grantees, the
circumstances considered as breach or default and the sanctions to be
imposed in such cases; and
c. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to submit explanation/
justification why there were no scholars selected from the other eleven (11)
barangays and the uneven distribution of scholars per barangay.
d. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit a report on the
individual profile of the ninety one (91) scholars to determine whether these
beneficiaries fall within the category of poor and deserving students residing
within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman and
the BAC members to submit justification why the contract was awarded to a
General Building Contractor when the transaction involved was a supply of
information technology equipment.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the
provisions of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of
Education, Department of Budget and Management and Department of the
Interior and Local Government in the utilization of Special Education Fund
(SEF). We further recommended that the Municipality adhere stringently to
the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No.
9184 in the procurement of local government goods.

We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the provisions of
Republic Act No. 9003 or “Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act
of 2000” which objectives are to protect public health and the environment
through ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the
provision of Section 5(2) of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 of the Philippine Commission on Women, Department of the
Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and
National Economic and Development Authority that the municipality shall
submit her Annual Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB)
to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial
Government to ensure the alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the
priorities of the province and then to the DILG Provincial Office for its review
and endorsement to the concerned LGU for incorporation in her annual budget
to be enacted by her Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal
officials ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is
primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes. Any delay in the completion of development projects means to
slow down community development or realization of development objectives.

We recommended that the Municipality submit justification on the
reason why there was a delay in the creation of the Council and explanation on
the implementation of the programs and activities in spite of the absence of an
authorized body in charge of the program pursuant toSection 15 of Republic Act
No. 9344 or the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members
of the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building
for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of
documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality,
particularly those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the
preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply the BIR regulations on
the withholding and remittance of taxes on compensation to avoid penalties and
surcharges to be charged against concerned officials found to be at fault.

We recommended that the Municipality remit the due consolidated loan
amortizations pursuant to Section 14.2, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Government Service Insurance Act of 1997 or R.A. No. 8291 and
avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed
penalties.

In view of the foregoing, we respectfully recommended that the Local
Chief Executive require the General Services Officer to have all motor vehicles
owned by the Municipality be marked with "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" and
the name of the Municipal Government of Calaca or Municipality of Calaca
written below it to properly identify the vehicles as government property and
avoid audit disallowance as directed under Paragraph 3, Item VII of COA
Circular No. 75-6.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully comply with
the aforementioned law, rules and regulations of government accounting and
auditing on the submission of the year-end financial statements particularly
of the 40 barangays of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and maintain a
complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and prepare and submit
financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and
110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipality particularly the Municipal Mayor
and the Municipal Engineer recoup the excessive contract cost amounting to
P116,372.52 in connection with the implementation of the infrastructure project;
Concreting of Sinisian Farm to Market Road at Barangay Sinisian, Calaca,
Batangas from the contractor Juben Contruction pursuant to Section 31.1 of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. (R.A.) 9184,
otherwise known as the “Government Procurement Reform Act.”

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
 The Inventory Team to conduct complete Report on the inventory-taking of all the LGU’s properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:


  • The Inventory Team to conduct  complete  inventory-taking  of  all  the
    LGU’s properties and prepare  the  Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of
    Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor  concerned
    not later than January 31 of each year; and

  • The Municipal Accountant  and  the  GSO/Property  Officer  to  maintain
    complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively,
    to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.


      The Municipal  Accountant,  Municipal  Engineer  and  General  Service
    Officer informed that the Municipal Mayor had  already  instructed  them  to
    conduct inventory of Infrastructure Assets of the Municipality.

      On the other hand the Municipal Accountant and  the  General  Services
    Officer submitted/reconciled Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of  Property,
    Plant and Equipment for Calendar Year  2016.  According  to  them  the  huge
    variance that was reconciled was from the account of  Water  Supply  System,
    Other Infrastructure Asset, School Buildings and Other Structures.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  prepare  regularly  the
      monthly Statement of Bank Reconciliation.   The  statement  is  one  of  the
      fundamental elements of internal control on cash. Also, the  account  should
      be presented in the cashbook and in the ledger always accurately and  up  to
      date every month and yearly.


      The Municipal Accountant explained that she was not  able  to  prepare
      regularly the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement due to  workload  volume
      but she pointed out that she will prepare it yearly.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
      Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the  Municipal
      Accountant to make  proper  adjustments  in  the  municipality’s  books  of
      accounts to fairly present its financial  statements  as  of  December  31,
      2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal
      Treasurer expedite the submission of the  request  for  write-off  of  these
      dormant accounts to the  Commission  on  Audit  supported  with  Sangguniang
      Bayan Resolution requesting the same  and  certification  as  to  extent  of
      management validation  on  these  accounts,  aging  schedule  and  available
      records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.

      The  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  positively
      answered that  they  will  prepare  the  necessary  certification  and  will
      request from the Sanggunian Bayan a resolution for  write-off  of  the  said
      dormant account.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
      Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the long outstanding  Due
      to Other NGAs accounts amounting to  P2,003,599.07  and  effect  appropriate
      disposition/return  to  the  source  agency  or  to  the  National  Treasury
      pursuant  to Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7  of  COA
      Circular No. 94-013.


      The Municipal  Accountant and Municipal Treasurer  informed  that  the
      amount of  P383,004.97 had been remitted to Bureau of Treasury  under  check
      number  435083  dated  March  1,  2017,  while  the  remaining   amount   of
      P1,616,000.00 for BuB projects will be implemented early this Calendar  Year
      2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct  the  Municipal
      Accountant to record  expenses  according  to  the  corresponding  accounts
      prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.


       Likewise,  we  recommended  that  the  Honorable  Mayor  require  the
      Municipal Accountant to immediately submit  the  required  documents  noted
      therein and to stop the  practice  of  paying  disbursement  vouchers  with
      incomplete supporting documents. Strict compliance with Section 4(6) of  PD
      1445 is highly recommended.

      The Municipal Accountant assured that  she  will  submit  the  lacking
      documents and will record expenses according to the corresponding  accounts
      prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the  production  of  all
    legal documents from the Office of the Municipal Assessor for  the  issuance
    of  Tax  Declaration  in  the  name  of  the  Municipality  and   the   Land
    Registration Authority/Register of Deeds for the issuance of an  appropriate
    Certificate of Title/Transfer Certificate of Title.

      We also advised the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply  with  the
    requirements on the procurement of land as mandated in  Section  13  of  COA
    Circular No. 2012-001  and pertinent  provision of Presidential  Decree  No.
    1529.   Ensure that all claims against government funds  be  supported  with
    all  the  required  documentations.   Require  submission  of  all   lacking
    documents.  Refrain  from  paying  disbursement  vouchers  with   incomplete
    supporting documents.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the
      Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentation stated  therein  to
      ensure  validity  and  propriety  of  this   transaction,   otherwise,   the
      procurement shall not be allowed in audit.

      Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  require  the
      Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer and the  members  of  the
      BAC to explain why the procurement was made without  sufficient  budget  and
      inclusion in the Annual Procurement Plan. In addition, we  recommended  that
      the defaulting supplier be responsible for the payment of the  corresponding
      liquidated damages computed based on the number of delays incurred.

      We also advised the Municipal Accountant to ensure  that  all  claims
      against government funds are supported with all the required  documentations
      as required in Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.  Refrain from paying  disbursement
      vouchers with incomplete supporting documents.

       We also recommended that all procurements to be  undertaken  for  the
      year be included in the Approved  Annual  Procurement  Plan  supported  with
      sufficient  budget.   Ensure  that  notice  of  bidding  be   given   widest
      dissemination to encourage competitive bidders so that the Municipality  can
      avail of the best quality at lowest cost of items procured.

We   recommended   that   the   Municipality   implement    immediately
    program/projects  under  BuB   projects   for   the   development   of   its
    constituents.  We also recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  strictly
    comply with the requirements  set  forth  in  the  DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC  Joint
    Memorandum Circular No. 6 and COA Cir. No. 2012-001. Ensure that all  claims
    against government funds be  supported  with  all  the  required  documents.
    Require  submission  of  lacking   documents   and   refrain   from   paying
    disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
    Treasurer  and  the  OIC-Market  Administrator  to  vigorously  enforce  the
    provisions of the Contract of  Lease  to  collect  the  delinquent  accounts
    amounting to P6,687,256.00,  otherwise,  file  the  necessary  legal  action
    against defaulting stallholders.


      Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive and  concerned
    municipal officials conduct review  and  evaluation  of  the  public  market
    operational performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned  officials
    stop the practice of procuring infrastructure without the approved Annual
    Procurement Plan and Project Procurement Management  Plan.   Otherwise,  any
    procurement not included in the approved Annual Procurement Plan  (APP)  and
    without Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) shall not be  allowed  in
    audit.


      Likewise, require all the department heads to prepare and submit their
    respective PPMP for  their  programs,  activities  and  projects  (PAPs)  to
    support the submitted APP.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  ensure  the  efficient  and
    effective  utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  which  is  primarily
    intended to fund  projects  that  shall  contribute  to  the  attainment  of
    desirable socio-economic development and environmental management  outcomes.
    We  further  recommended  that  required  development  planning  shall  be
    observed particularly the enumeration of specific  development  projects  or
    activities  and  the  itemization  of  its  appropriation.   Likewise,   the
    Municipality shall implement the provisions of  COA  Circular  No.  2013-004
    dated January 30, 2013  to publicize the  projects/programs/activities  or
    notify the public on its essential features/elements  in  the  conspicuous
    places within the agency premises and in the project venue.

We recommended that the Municipality fully  implement  the  provisions
    of Republic Act No. 9003 or “Philippine Ecological  Solid  Waste  Management
    Act of 2000” that espouses on the implementation of the 3R  or  the  reduce,
    reuse and recycle concept with focus on reduction  of  pollution  and  waste
    generation.

We recommended  that  the  Municipality  pursue  gender  mainstreaming
    completely  in  the  locality.  Establish  the  GAD  Database   and  mainstream
    gender perspectives in the local development plans to  institutionalize  the
    processes/mechanisms in the implementation of the Magna Carta  of Women  in
    the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the official concern
    to properly utilize the fund for  the  implementation  of  programs/projects
    under Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund(LDRRMF) for the  benefit
    of  constituents particularly in the affected and vulnerable  areas  and  to
    refer to Section 5.1.10  of COA Circular No. 2012-002  dated September 12,
    2012 and Section 21 of Republic Act 10121 for the  recording  and  reporting
    of LDRRMF and use of 30 percent and 70 percent calamity fund.

      We also recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare subsidiary ledger
    by year for prior years’ unexpended balances of the fund under  the  account
    Trust Liability  –  DRRMF  and  the  Treasurer  transfer  the  current  year’s
    unexpended balance of the QRF and  Mitigation Fund at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly  comply  with  the
    provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No.  01  s.  1998  of
    the Department of Budget  and  Management  (DBM),  Department  of  Education
    Culture  and  Sports  (DECS)  and  Department  of  the  Interior  and  Local
    Government (DILG) in order to ensure that Special Education Fund (SEF)  will
    be utilized for the intended purpose and to  promote  quality  education  in
    every public schools in the  Municipality  of  Calaca.   The  non-chargeable
    expenses are disallowed in audit and shall be refunded by the officials  who
    allowed the incurrence of  expenditures  not  included  in  the  prioritized
    expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  provide and  maintain
    a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and   prepare  and
    submit  financial statements as required in  Section  313  of  RA  7160  and
    Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
    Engineer to closely  coordinate  with  the  respective  contractors  of  the
    projects mentioned and act accordingly on the cost of deficiencies noted  by
    the   COA   Engineers   in   order   to    effect    immediately    possible
    corrections/adjustments and inform the audit team of the courses of  actions
    undertaken by the concerned contractors  for  information  to  the  COA-TAS.
    Likewise, impose  liquidated  damages  to  the  defaulting  contractor  with
    regard to the project delays totaling 450 calendar days.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementation of the following audit recommendations:

The Inventory Team conduct complete inventory-taking of all the LGU’s properties and prepare  the  Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and

The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records. 
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the cost and corresponding depreciation of the properties procured out of BUB to fairly present the PPE accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Team started the physical count last October 2016






PPE Ledger and Property Cards were started updated in October 2016




Adjusted per JEV No. 16120122 dated Dec. 31, 2016.

The Municipal Accountant and the GSO submitted/reconciled Inventory, of Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2016.  However, discrepancies remained in some accounts.  




Updating ongoing

Make the necessary adjustments in the books for the difference of P50,115,489.92 for real property and special education tax receivables to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:

Also, direct the LDDRM Officer to prepare the correct annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and submit regularly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply always with the explicit conditions imposed by regulations on the utilization of funds in accordance with authorized appropriations and strictly monitor the availability of funds and appropriations to avoid incurrence of illegal expenditures and illegal use of public funds.

Also, observe strictly Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular (CSC MC) No. 17 and 24 series of 2002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, respectively. Require concerned local officials and employees to submit all the lacking documentary requirements for the payments to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe provisions under the Department of Health Administrative Order No. 163 series of 2002 in the procurement of its drugs and medicines.  Ensure that only those drugs and medicines that conform with and listed in the Essential Drugs List of the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) should be requested and procured, otherwise, the requesting official should request with written justification to the National Formulary Committee for the exceptions, to avoid disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement strictly provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority to institutionalize gender mainstreaming mechanisms/processes in the LGU to ensure that women’s as well as men’s concerns and experiences are integral to the design, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of all legislation, policies and programs so that women and men benefit equally and inequality is not perpetuated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to unauthorized officials/employees and adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, R. A. 7160 and P. D. 1445; and
